Deep clustering methods draw attention from researchers, as mentioned in references [1, 9, 40, 50–52].
Deep clustering methods learn feature presentations of each view using a view-specific encoder network.
Deep clustering methods fuse the learned representations from all views to obtain a consensus representation using a clustering module.
Some methods align representation or label distributions from different views by KL divergence.
Some methods align representation from different views by contrastive learning.
Existing deep MVC methods are based on view-wise fusion models such as weighted-sum fusion or concatenating fusion of all views.
Existing deep MVC methods struggle to obtain discriminative consensus representations from multiple views due to noise or missing data in the collection process.
Alignment methods used in existing deep MVC methods aim to improve the model based on view-wise fusion models.
Alignment methods, such as contrastive learning, usually distinguish positive and negative pairs from the sample-level.
The alignment methods based on contrastive learning might conflict with the clustering objective, where representations of samples from the same cluster should be similar to each other.
The multi-view representation learning framework for clustering is proposed to alleviate the problems related to alignment methods and contrastive learning.
Representations of samples from the same category are typically similar, which is a key insight in learning consensus data representation from multi-view data.
In contrastive learning, we should increase the similarity of view representations from the same cluster, not only from the same sample, which benefits clustering tasks.

The proposed module is a flexible multi-view data representation module that can be applied to incomplete multi-view data clustering tasks by enhancing the consensus representation of a sample with missing view data using samples with high structure relationships.
The proposed module can be plugged into the framework of other methods for incomplete multi-view data clustering tasks.
Experiments show that the proposed method achieves excellent performance in both complete and incomplete multi-view clustering tasks.
In the existing representation learning-based MVC family, two kinds of methods are shallow representation learning-based method and deep representation learning-based method.
Shallow representation learning-based multi-view clustering methods are categorized into two types: multi-view graph clustering and multi-view subspace clustering.

The multiview contrastive learning module learns a consensus representation by considering global structure relationships among samples, fully exploring the complementary nature of similar samples.

The multiview contrastive learning module integrates the learnt global structure relationship and consensus representation into contrastive learning, making data representations in the same cluster similar.

In this paper, we propose a novel multi-view representation learning network for clustering.
We design a GCFAgg module, which can learn a global similarity relationship among samples, and enhance a consensus representation by all samples’ representation.
Representations of samples with high structure relationship are made more similar.
The SgCL module is designed to address the problem of inconsistent representations from different samples in the same cluster.
SgCL module improves on the inconsistency issue present in previous contrastive learning methods.
Experimental results show that the approach has state-of-the-art performance in both complete and incomplete MVC tasks.
